{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Operations Manager",
  "description": "GWX is a 2nd, 3rd &amp; 4th party consumer-oriented supply chain management company with core competence in courier delivery service, haulage, logistics, air cargo services, service quality audit, third party verification, international businesses and customs clearing &amp; handling.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job&nbsp;Description


	GWX Logistics Limited is seeking a strategic and results-driven Operations Manager to lead and optimize our end-to-end logistics operations across courier, haulage, and cargo services.
	In this role, you will oversee daily operations, ensuring timely and efficient service delivery while proactively resolving issues that may impact performance.
	You will lead and manage cross-functional teams, including fleet, dispatch, and field staff, setting clear expectations and driving productivity through coaching and performance management.
	You will identify operational gaps and implement process improvements, including developing and enforcing standard operating procedures to enhance efficiency in routing, delivery timelines, and resource utilization.
	A strong focus will also be placed on customer experience, ensuring high service standards and resolving escalated issues in collaboration with customer service teams.
	Additionally, you will manage operational costs, monitor key performance metrics, and provide data-driven insights to improve efficiency and profitability. You will ensure compliance with regulatory requirements and safety standards while working closely with internal teams to align operations with business growth and strategic objectives.
	This role is ideal for an experienced professional with strong leadership, analytical, and problem-solving skills, and a proven ability to drive operational excellence in a fast-paced logistics environment.


Salary
N250,000 - N300,000 monthly.&nbsp;

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;jobs@greaterwashingtonng.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

  "validThrough": "2026-04-23",

Operations Manager Job at GWX Limited




        
    

        
        GWX is a 2nd, 3rd & 4th party consumer-oriented supply chain management company with core competence in courier delivery service, haulage, logistics, air cargo services, service quality audit, third party verification, international businesses and customs clearing & handling.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Manager

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	GWX Logistics Limited is seeking a strategic and results-driven Operations Manager to lead and optimize our end-to-end logistics operations across courier, haulage, and cargo services.
	In this role, you will oversee daily operations, ensuring timely and efficient service delivery while proactively resolving issues that may impact performance.
	You will lead and manage cross-functional teams, including fleet, dispatch, and field staff, setting clear expectations and driving productivity through coaching and performance management.
	You will identify operational gaps and implement process improvements, including developing and enforcing standard operating procedures to enhance efficiency in routing, delivery timelines, and resource utilization.
	A strong focus will also be placed on customer experience, ensuring high service standards and resolving escalated issues in collaboration with customer service teams.
	Additionally, you will manage operational costs, monitor key performance metrics, and provide data-driven insights to improve efficiency and profitability. You will ensure compliance with regulatory requirements and safety standards while working closely with internal teams to align operations with business growth and strategic objectives.
	This role is ideal for an experienced professional with strong leadership, analytical, and problem-solving skills, and a proven ability to drive operational excellence in a fast-paced logistics environment.


Salary
N250,000 - N300,000 monthly. 

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Graphic Designer",
  "description": "Since our establishment, Primeguage Solutions Limited have become the fastest-growing revenue management and technology consulting firm in Nigeria. Driven by passion and hard work, we help our clients build stronger, more agile, and innovative businesses. Rather than dwell on our past achievements, we look forward to the enormous opportunities that are emerging from the growing shift to digital business. We invest in new solutions and acquisitions, build our capabilities, and develop our teams to ensure that Primeguage is well-positioned to help clients explore the vast opportunity &ndash; and deliver on the potential &ndash; of this new digital business era.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Graphic Designer

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Design professional documents, reports, proposals, and presentations using Canva and PowerPoint
	Create visually appealing presentations for meetings, pitches, and events
	Maintain consistency with the organization&rsquo;s branding and style guides
	Edit and improve existing materials to enhance visual appeal and clarity
	Work with the team to understand project needs and deliver on time


Requirements


	BSc or HND in any field (Design, Marketing, Communications, or related fields is an advantage.
	Strong design skills and creativity
	Proficiency in Canva and Microsoft PowerPoint
	Good understanding of layouts, colors, and document formatting
	Attention to detail and ability to follow branding guidelines
	Ability to work independently and meet deadlines


Nice to Have:


	Familiarity with other design tools or platforms
	Experience designing corporate or proposal documents
	Behavioral Competence and Requirements:
	Strong communication and collaboration skills, ability to work closely with researchers, writers, and executives.
	Highly organized and capable of managing multiple documents and projects simultaneously.
	Intellectual curiosity and willingness to learn subject matter to improve visual storytelling.
	Adaptable and responsive to iterative feedback in a fast-paced environment.
	Commitment to deadlines and high editorial/design standards.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified&nbsp;candidates should send their CV and portfolio to:&nbsp;recruitment@primeguage.co&nbsp;using &quot;Application for Graphic Desinger Role&quot; as the subject of the email.",

  },

Graphic Designer Job at Primeguage Solutions Limited




        
    

        
        Since our establishment, Primeguage Solutions Limited have become the fastest-growing revenue management and technology consulting firm in Nigeria. Driven by passion and hard work, we help our clients build stronger, more agile, and innovative businesses. Rather than dwell on our past achievements, we look forward to the enormous opportunities that are emerging from the growing shift to digital business. We invest in new solutions and acquisitions, build our capabilities, and develop our teams to ensure that Primeguage is well-positioned to help clients explore the vast opportunity – and deliver on the potential – of this new digital business era.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Graphic Designer
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Design professional documents, reports, proposals, and presentations using Canva and PowerPoint
	Create visually appealing presentations for meetings, pitches, and events
	Maintain consistency with the organization’s branding and style guides
	Edit and improve existing materials to enhance visual appeal and clarity
	Work with the team to understand project needs and deliver on time


Requirements


	BSc or HND in any field (Design, Marketing, Communications, or related fields is an advantage.
	Strong design skills and creativity
	Proficiency in Canva and Microsoft PowerPoint
	Good understanding of layouts, colors, and document formatting
	Attention to detail and ability to follow branding guidelines
	Ability to work independently and meet deadlines


Nice to Have:


	Familiarity with other design tools or platforms
	Experience designing corporate or proposal documents
	Behavioral Competence and Requirements:
	Strong communication and collaboration skills, ability to work closely with researchers, writers, and executives.
	Highly organized and capable of managing multiple documents and projects simultaneously.
	Intellectual curiosity and willingness to learn subject matter to improve visual storytelling.
	Adaptable and responsive to iterative feedback in a fast-paced environment.
	Commitment to deadlines and high editorial/design standards.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and portfolio to: [email protected] using "Application for Graphic Desinger Role" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

  "description": "Worknigeria was established with a Singular Vision. To help the vast Nigerian workforce find meaningful jobs. We are also on a mission to upskill them in the best ways possible and help them grow with and within their careers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Assistant HSE Manager - Depot
Job Location:&nbsp;Ijegun, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Sector: Oil &amp; Gas / Energy
Work Structure: Fulltime, Onsite

Role Summary


	The Assistant HSE Manager will support the development, implementation, and maintenance of the company&#39;s health, safety, and environmental (HSE) programs across its retail, depot, and office operations.
	The role involves assisting in overseeing HSE activities, conducting risk assessments, ensuring compliance with regulations, and driving continuous improvement through data-driven analytics.


Key Responsibilities


	Assist in developing, implementing, and maintaining HSE policies, procedures, and standards.
	Conduct and support risk assessments; recommend and implement control measures.
	Monitor compliance with local, national, and international HSE regulations.
	Support in planning and conducting safety audits and preparing audit reports.
	Participate in incident investigations; assist in root cause analysis and recommending corrective actions.
	Maintain incident records and ensure timely reporting in line with company policies.
	Support in developing and delivering HSE training programs and safety awareness initiatives.
	Ensure personnel are trained in emergency response procedures and proper use of PPE.
	Monitor environmental compliance, including waste management and pollution control.
	Contribute to identifying and driving continuous improvement initiatives using analytics.
	Promote a culture of safety by acting as a role model in HSE practices.
	Support the development and coordination of emergency response plans and drills.


Job Requirements


	Bachelors degree in Environmental Science, Occupational Health &amp; Safety, Engineering, or a related discipline.
	7-10 years of relevant HSE experience in a Depot within the oil &amp; gas sector.
	A Masters degree or professional certifications (e.g., NEBOSH, IOSH, ISPON HSE Level 3) are preferred.
	Minimum of 3years in a supervisory or team lead role.
	Strong knowledge of HSE management systems, risk assessments, incident investigations, and HSE analytics.
	In-depth knowledge of oil &amp; gas industry HSE regulations and standards.
	Ability to analyze trends and recommend process improvements.


Salary
N600,000 - N1,00,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;careers@worknigeria.com&nbsp;using the Job Position and Location as the subject of the email.

Assistant HSE Manager - Depot Job at Worknigeria




        
    

        
        Worknigeria was established with a Singular Vision. To help the vast Nigerian workforce find meaningful jobs. We are also on a mission to upskill them in the best ways possible and help them grow with and within their careers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Assistant HSE Manager - Depot
Job Location: Ijegun, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Sector: Oil & Gas / Energy
Work Structure: Fulltime, Onsite

Role Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Assistant HSE Manager will support the development, implementation, and maintenance of the company's health, safety, and environmental (HSE) programs across its retail, depot, and office operations.
	The role involves assisting in overseeing HSE activities, conducting risk assessments, ensuring compliance with regulations, and driving continuous improvement through data-driven analytics.


Key Responsibilities


	Assist in developing, implementing, and maintaining HSE policies, procedures, and standards.
	Conduct and support risk assessments; recommend and implement control measures.
	Monitor compliance with local, national, and international HSE regulations.
	Support in planning and conducting safety audits and preparing audit reports.
	Participate in incident investigations; assist in root cause analysis and recommending corrective actions.
	Maintain incident records and ensure timely reporting in line with company policies.
	Support in developing and delivering HSE training programs and safety awareness initiatives.
	Ensure personnel are trained in emergency response procedures and proper use of PPE.
	Monitor environmental compliance, including waste management and pollution control.
	Contribute to identifying and driving continuous improvement initiatives using analytics.
	Promote a culture of safety by acting as a role model in HSE practices.
	Support the development and coordination of emergency response plans and drills.


Job Requirements


	Bachelors degree in Environmental Science, Occupational Health & Safety, Engineering, or a related discipline.
	7-10 years of relevant HSE experience in a Depot within the oil & gas sector.
	A Masters degree or professional certifications (e.g., NEBOSH, IOSH, ISPON HSE Level 3) are preferred.
	Minimum of 3years in a supervisory or team lead role.
	Strong knowledge of HSE management systems, risk assessments, incident investigations, and HSE analytics.
	In-depth knowledge of oil & gas industry HSE regulations and standards.
	Ability to analyze trends and recommend process improvements.


Salary
N600,000 - N1,00,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position and Location as the subject of the email.

Note: Only candidates with Depot HSE experience will be contacted

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Creative Assistant",
  "description": "Emador Nigeria Integrated Resources is a dynamic, multi-sector business dedicated to delivering innovative and reliable solutions across the energy, real estate, and general trading industries. The company specializes in solar energy sales and services, providing sustainable and cost-effective power solutions to homes and businesses.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Creative Assistant

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos

Job Summary


	We are looking for a Creative Assistant to join our Marketing team and participate in various branding projects.


Responsibilities


	Help design promotional material for events, like brochures and videos.
	Prepare content for internal use (like guidelines and glossaries).
	Create customizable templates we can use for multiple purposes, including presentations and business cards.
	Write copy for marketing and communication material, including press releases, infographics and social media posts.
	Coordinate with team members to gather images, logos, stats and content to use in various creative projects.
	Maintain updated records of the videos, images and guides that we use.
	Research and recommend new ideas for strengthening our brand.


Requirements


	Understanding of digital advertising tactics.
	Copywriting skills.
	Excellent written and verbal communication abilities.


Salary
N100,000 - N150,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;integratedresourcesemadorniger@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position&nbsp; as the subject of the mail.",

Creative Assistant Job at Emador Nigeria Integrated Resources




        
    

        
        Emador Nigeria Integrated Resources is a dynamic, multi-sector business dedicated to delivering innovative and reliable solutions across the energy, real estate, and general trading industries. The company specializes in solar energy sales and services, providing sustainable and cost-effective power solutions to homes and businesses.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Creative Assistant

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos

Job Summary


	We are looking for a Creative Assistant to join our Marketing team and participate in various branding projects.


Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Help design promotional material for events, like brochures and videos.
	Prepare content for internal use (like guidelines and glossaries).
	Create customizable templates we can use for multiple purposes, including presentations and business cards.
	Write copy for marketing and communication material, including press releases, infographics and social media posts.
	Coordinate with team members to gather images, logos, stats and content to use in various creative projects.
	Maintain updated records of the videos, images and guides that we use.
	Research and recommend new ideas for strengthening our brand.


Requirements


	Understanding of digital advertising tactics.
	Copywriting skills.
	Excellent written and verbal communication abilities.


Salary
N100,000 - N150,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position  as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Operations Support Officer",
  "description": "Fort Knox Outsourcing was set up to cater to the growing need of businesses in Nigeria to reap the economic, strategic and operational benefits offered by outsourcing. We act as a &ldquo;Third Party Service Provider&rdquo; that Source, Select, and Second employment ready workforce in line with client specifications to meet their talent needs as quickly as possible!

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Support Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Provide administrative support to the operations team.
	Coordinate daily operational activities and ensure timely execution.
	Maintain accurate records, reports, and documentation.
	Assist in monitoring staff performance and compliance with company policies.
	Support logistics, inventory tracking, and resource allocation.
	Liaise with internal teams and external stakeholders as required.
	Handle basic client or vendor inquiries and escalate when necessary.
	Ensure adherence to operational procedures and standards.


Requirements


	Minimum of HND or B.Sc Degree in any related field.
	1&ndash;2 years of experience in an operations or administrative role.
	Strong organizational and multitasking skills.
	Good communication and interpersonal skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools (Word, Excel, etc.).
	Ability to work with minimal supervision and meet deadlines.
	Must be resident in Abuja or willing to relocate.


Salary
N150,000 Net monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;recruitment@fortknoxoutsourcing.com&nbsp;using the Job Position and Location as subject of the mail.&nbsp;",

Operations Support Officer Job at Fort Knox Outsourcing




        
    

        
        Fort Knox Outsourcing was set up to cater to the growing need of businesses in Nigeria to reap the economic, strategic and operational benefits offered by outsourcing. We act as a “Third Party Service Provider” that Source, Select, and Second employment ready workforce in line with client specifications to meet their talent needs as quickly as possible!

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Support Officer

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Provide administrative support to the operations team.
	Coordinate daily operational activities and ensure timely execution.
	Maintain accurate records, reports, and documentation.
	Assist in monitoring staff performance and compliance with company policies.
	Support logistics, inventory tracking, and resource allocation.
	Liaise with internal teams and external stakeholders as required.
	Handle basic client or vendor inquiries and escalate when necessary.
	Ensure adherence to operational procedures and standards.


Requirements


	Minimum of HND or B.Sc Degree in any related field.
	1–2 years of experience in an operations or administrative role.
	Strong organizational and multitasking skills.
	Good communication and interpersonal skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools (Word, Excel, etc.).
	Ability to work with minimal supervision and meet deadlines.
	Must be resident in Abuja or willing to relocate.


Salary
N150,000 Net monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position and Location as subject of the mail.
